# Rebecca Windrow (Margo)

Rebecca Windrow

Rebecca Windrow grew up in Baldur's Gate with her father. Her father Callen Windrow was an old time riverman, delivering goods between cities for a price. A delivery man.   
Mr. Windrow taught his daughter how to be a proper riverman sailing downstream and upstream with important packages.  
Rebecca grew up traveling up and down on the river, or attending school in Baldur's Gate. But they grew up poor, scavenging by. When things got worse Mr. Windrow resorted to hunting. Rebecca learned to hunt with a crossbow, which was always a lot steadier when shooting from a rowboat.   
   
One day, the simple life ended as her father grew sick. Whatever supposed insurance Mr. Windrow had soon was bankrupt and didn’t pay a single coin. With no money for medicine Rebecca sailed to Candlekeep over the windy sea with a boat made for rivers to buy medicine. With no money left, she tried to smuggle it in. She got caught. Authorities confiscated the smuggled goods and Rebecca’s father died.

Politics, nobles, rich aristocrats and the city’s guards \- all corrupt assholes. Rebecca returned to her life of delivering merchandise. However, now a new goal was born.   
Illegal immigrants that the guards will shun away, orphans running hungry and sick workers dying from colds. Rebecca smuggles in everything needed for those shunned away from society. But of course one needs money, she also delivers merchandise for the highest bidder (legal).

At 25 years old she has some connections. 

1\. The rival. Best and worst part of the week is meeting Franceska Riverun. A fellow smuggler. But she’s always faster, more cunning, her boat is faster and prettier and she can charm her way through every customer. That is when she’s not trying to drown Rebecca’s poor old boat.  
Rebecca hates her and envies her. 

2\. Pagal sita: Baldur's Gate | Forgotten Realms Wiki | Fandom  
Yra dbr kazkoks Guild in Baldur’s Gate?

The tax collectors \- Every merchandise brought into Baldur's Gate has to go through tolls. The guild knows about Rebecca’s smuggling and let her pass without a word. Will one day the guild come to collect what they’re owed? Will they ask her to smuggle something in? Rebecca knows they can’t be doing that for the goodness of their heart.  
But one of the guild members, that is stationed at the docks, always smiles as Rebecca passes and doesn’t check her crates or barrels. Also, at least he does not let Franceska pass without tolls.   
\#Romance The guild member always smiles when Rebecca passes through the docks. (Does not have to be Astarion. Would like a guy with black hair? Finance, 6.5, blue eyes. \<3 With a tragic backstory of course.)

3\. Friends with an orphanage director/coordinator (nezinau kaip pavadint). Rebecca brings food, clothes as often as she can. Spends her free time helping to take care of the children.

4\. Knows the barkeep of one of the inns in Baldur’s Gate. Perhaps the Low Lantern? A three story ship turned into a tavern. If the DM allows: Rebecca’s great great grandfather sailed with that ship. (Add how many greats you want.) In the wiki it is written that in this tavern people gamble a lot. It was Rebecca’s father’s favourite place and Rebecca is a bit of a gambler. One of her more shameful qualities. Perhaps she knows the barkeep there, a friend of her father. Would like a discount for ale please.

Motivations:   
When Elturel, a city she has brought merchandise to and from many times, descends into hell. Along with it goes her precious boat. It’s old, scrappy, but it does the job and it was her fathers. Along with the ship could go down the merchandise of a loyal customer. A customer, who’s goods Rebecca didn’t check. Could it be a magical \+3 crossbow? Who knows…

Alignment: Chaotic good.  
Rebecca Windrow wants to do good, but her ways of doing good might be deemed criminal by some. Smuggling is illegal.   
Rebecca dislikes politics, nobles, rich folk and guards. She is wary of the guild, except that one guy that smiles at her. She is envious of her rival and wants to be better. Rebecca has a gambling problem.

She is Fidgety (high dex), uninformed (dump stat int), pedantic and attentive. She is also helpful but a bit boastful. 

flaw

1. Gambling. Don't know when to stop, enjoy it too much even when I know it's not what a good person should do.  
2. Pedantic. I can't sleep without checking that my boat is securely docked. Can't hunt without proper cleaning of my crossbow. Sometimes I take too long to finish tasks because of this.

Fear:

1. Simple fear \- getting in trouble with authorities.  
2. Simple fear 2 \- afraid that the guild will come to collect one day.  
3. Afraid to meet the same fate or that someone close to me will meet the same fate as my father and I won't be able to do anything about it. Or simpler \- afraid to be helpless.

Background:  
![][image1]

### **Final Fusion: True River Guardian – Ascendant Form**

Once Rebecca merges with the four sacred Sahuagin scales and the full tablet is assembled into the Seal of the Maw, the ancient relic bursts into brilliant oceanic light, and the essence of Sekolah’s Maw fuses with her. In that moment, she becomes the **True River Guardian**, endowed with abilities akin to a very rare magical item, yet burdened by a greater risk of chaos.

#### **Base Fusion Powers (Permanent, Post-Merger)**

* **Shark Life Ferocity**

  * **Natural Bite:** Rebecca gains a natural bite attack that deals 1d10 piercing damage plus 1d8 cold damage.  
  * **Predatory Instincts:** She has advantage on melee attacks against creatures below their hit point maximum.  
* **Enhanced Blood Sense**

  * **Tracking Blood:** Rebecca can detect any creature that has lost hit points within 60 feet when in aquatic environments, granting advantage on Perception and Survival checks for tracking.  
* **Tidal Magic Mastery**

  * **Innate Spellcasting:**  
    * At will: *shape water*  
    * Once per day each: *control water* and *tidal wave* (spell save DC 16, \+8 to hit)  
  * **Tidal Surge (1/Long Rest):** As an action, she can unleash a 20-foot cone of surging water. Each creature in the area must make a DC 16 Strength saving throw, taking 5d8 bludgeoning damage on a failed save (half on success) and being knocked prone or pushed 10 feet if underwater.  
* **Enhanced Aquatic Adaptations**

  * **Improved Swim Speed:** Her swim speed now equals **double her walking speed**.  
  * **Underwater Mastery:** She can breathe underwater and moves effortlessly through turbulent, flooded environments.  
  * **Cold Resistance:** She gains resistance to cold damage permanently.  
* **Guardian’s Resilience**

  * **Defensive Reaction:** Once per short rest, when she or an ally within 10 feet is hit, she can impose disadvantage on the attack.  
  * **Predatory Recovery:** When she reduces a creature to 0 hit points with her natural attacks, she gains temporary hit points equal to her character level.  
* **Risk of Blood Frenzy**

  * Whenever a creature within 30 feet is reduced to 0 hit points or she takes 20+ damage in a round, she must succeed on a DC 15 Wisdom saving throw or enter a state of **Blood Frenzy** for 1 minute. While frenzied, she gains \+2 bonus damage on melee attacks but is compelled to attack the nearest enemy, potentially causing friendly fire. She can attempt the saving throw at the end of each of her turns to end the frenzy.

---

#### **Avatar of Sekolah Transformation (Once Every 7–10 Days)**

Rebecca can channel the full, overwhelming power of Sekolah by transforming into the **Avatar of Sekolah** for 1 minute. To activate this transformation, she must willingly sacrifice part of her vitality (she takes 10 temporary hit points damage upon activation) and make a DC 18 Constitution saving throw. On a failure, the transformation is unstable, and she suffers one level of exhaustion at the end of the transformation.

**While Transformed, she gains the following for 1 minute:**

* **Doubled Aquatic Speed:** Her swim speed is quadrupled relative to her walking speed.  
* **Enhanced Attack Prowess:**  
  * All melee attacks are made with advantage, and her natural bite now deals 2d10 piercing plus 2d8 cold damage.  
* **Legendary Fury:**  
  * She gains one additional legendary action option that she can use at the end of other creatures’ turns, such as a bonus “Frenzied Charge” that deals an extra 2d6 damage.  
* **Tidal Dominion:**  
  * She can cast *control water* as a bonus action during the transformation without expending a spell slot.

**Transformation Drawback:**  
 After the transformation ends, Rebecca must succeed on a DC 18 Wisdom save or remain under the Maw’s influence, resulting in a permanent scar (e.g., a lingering level of exhaustion that lasts until a long rest or additional in-game ritual is performed) and a recurring risk of triggering Blood Frenzy in future combat encounters.

---

### **Narrative & Visual Flavor**

When the fusion occurs, Rebecca’s skin begins to shimmer with ghostly blue-green runes. Her eyes take on an intense, predatory gleam, and subtle shark-like fins appear along her forearms and back. The Seal of the Maw—engraved into her chest—glows as it channels the ancient, chaotic power of Sekolah. In moments of dire need, she can invoke the Avatar transformation, her form shifting into a terrifying blend of human and monstrous shark—swift, powerful, and deadly—but at the cost of risking her humanity through uncontrollable fury.

This transformation is both a blessing and a curse, granting her unmatched power over the tides and the creatures of the deep, while also threatening to overwhelm her with the insatiable hunger of the Maw if she does not maintain her will.

# Drenval (Aistis)

Drenval‘s beginnings are nothing out of the ordinary. He grew up in the Dock District of the lower city, in a family of 4\. Drenval’s mother, Mara, was a kind soul, she was a devout follower of Helm and a herbalist by trade, Drenval spent most of his early childhood in her apothecary, learning the craft. Drenval’s Brother, Dumal, was a strict paladin, serving the Hellriders and worshiping Helm. Drenvalnever knew his father and his mother is even afraid to talk about him. To continue his legacy, Drenval, against the wishes of his mother, had joined the clergy of Helm’s Shieldhall, where he trained as an acolyte for more than a decade. He intended to join the Hellriders straight away, yet he was not blessed with a mighty build, and could not even don a suit of plate, so he had to choose the clergy instead. He was a good fit for them, as he was naturally curious and gifted. Over the years he became the perfect priest – calm and collected, loyal, and knowledgeable. It was time for him to fulfil his destiny and join the Hellriders, as a cleric apprentice. He has now been training as a Hellrider for several years and besides studying the lore books, training as a Watcher of the Fallen (his herbalist knowledge came in handy) and harnessing the divine powers, he had also gone on a few expeditions, where he could prove his worth. It was mostly simple encounters, but during one of them, Drenval had demonstrated his abilities, by using both divine magic and his herbalist knowledge to cure a comrade who had been poisoned by a goblin’s arrow. That person was Mohan and the 2 became good friends after that. He also finally got accepted by his peers and cemented his place in the 16th division.

* Drenval harbors a fear that he may fail to live up to his father’s legacy, especially since he cannot wield the same strength in battle. His skills and faith serve as his armor, but deep down, he wonders if it is enough. On the surface he maintains the facade that he is calm and ready.  
* Questionable circumstances of Dumal’s absence, and the lack of clarity regarding his disappearance from his parents still haunt Drenval. What became of his brother? Why does his mother avoid this topic?  
* Drenval never heard Helm’s voice, and though his presence is unquestionable, Drenval’s faith isn’t as strong as he claims it is

People of interest:

* Mara – Drenvals mother, caring herbalist, a bit reserved and secretive.  
* Dumal – missing brother.  
* Delryn – strict father, died in the line of duty?  
* Mohan – fellow hellrider, a bit fanatical, friend  
* Alaric – mentor, veteran cleric of Helm, Hellrider, Watcher of the Fallen, a father figure, devout follower, holy man

Potential story points translated:

* Drenval never learned how his father died, he was told he died in action and was well respected amongst peers, perhaps the truth is a bit different (maybe he was a drunken failure, maybe he rode into the hells themselves), and Drenvals identity would collapse if he learned the truth, or perhaps he would finally accept who he is.  
* Dumal might have joined a rival organization from another city, perhaps he somehow became part of an evil cult, or perhaps he is a well-respected person in Baldur’s gate.  
* Religiuos stuff – maybe a follower of lathander or illmater might convince Drenval to switch allegiance (and prepared spells), maybe there is a moment of divine intervention or symbolism, that makes Drenval the most devout follower of Helm.

![][image2]

flaw insecure apie savo physical strenght/savo place in the orders   
flaw is sort of afraid of mounts, and does his best to avoid one, which is especially akward because hes a hellrider   
flaw/afraid of making his own choice (always tries to follow the law and not go by his guts)   
afraid hes not gonna live up to his brothers legacy?, what happened to father   
afraid his faith is not as real/as strong as it is supposed to be  
afraid to stand head to head to an opponent, usually hides behind others

### **DM Guide: Drenval’s Awakening – The Whisper of Bhaal**

#### **Triggering the Event**

* Location: The Dungeon of the Dead Three, in the presence of a Bhaalite altar.  
* Catalyst: The *Crimson Rose of Bhaal* begins to pulse and glow when Drenval steps within 10 feet of the altar.  
* Effect: Drenval experiences a vision, a divine connection to Bhaal.

#### **The Vision**

1. **Pain and Darkness:** Drenval is wracked with sudden, unbearable pain. His vision blacks out as he collapses.  
2. **A Blood-Soaked Battlefield:** He sees warriors reveling in slaughter, a familiar shadowed figure standing in their midst.  
3. **His Mother’s Secrets:** A vision of Mara, his mother, begging unseen forces for mercy, whispering, *"He is safe… I swear it."*  
4. **A Corrupt Reflection:** He sees himself in Hellrider armor—then his holy symbol blackens, his eyes glow red, and a skull emblem brands his chest.  
5. **Bhaal’s Voice:**  
   * *"At last… you hear me."*  
   * *"You wear their chains, whisper their prayers, but I see your soul."*  
   * *"You are not weak. You are not afraid. You are mine."*  
   * *"Show me you are worthy. Let your blade carve truth into the world."*  
   * *"Do not disappoint me."*  
6. **Return to Reality:**  
   * The vision ends. Drenval collapses to the cold stone floor.  
   * The *Crimson Rose of Bhaal* bears a fresh drop of his blood.  
   * He feels an unnatural **urge to kill**.

#### **The Lingering Effect**

* **If he kills a creature within the next scene:** He gains **\+2 Wisdom and \+2 Dexterity for 24 hours**.  
* **If he resists the urge:** The hunger lingers within him, a gnawing temptation that will surface again.

**Conclusion:**  
Bhaal has made his presence known. The truth remains hidden, but the blood calls to Drenval. The Lord of Murder is watching.

### The chamber is thick with the stench of death and rot, the air almost suffocating. The flickering torchlight dances across the grotesque murals of the Dead Three’s deeds, each stroke of paint and dried blood telling of agony and sacrifice in their names.

The *Crimson Rose* pulses faintly in Drenval’s grip. A sickly red glow throbs in time with his heartbeat, as if it is alive. As he steps closer to the altar, the light intensifies, casting elongated shadows along the walls. The cultists falter in their ritual, eyes darting between each other in confusion and reverence, whispering prayers to the Lord of Murder.

Then—pain.

A lance of fire erupts through Drenval’s skull, as if unseen claws had gripped his mind and squeezed. His breath is stolen from him, his knees buckle, his hands tremble. The *Crimson Rose* is no longer simply glowing—it is burning, its light searing into his flesh, but he cannot let go. His fingers are locked around the stem, its thorns biting deep. The world around him shifts, distorts, and falls away.

A great silence swallows the room, devouring even the cries of the cultists.

Then, from the abyss, a voice like a dagger drawn from a sheath:

**"At last… you hear me."**

It is no mere whisper. It *echoes* in his bones, reverberating in his very blood, in the marrow of his soul.

His vision is no longer his own.

A battlefield stretches before him, bodies piled high, the soil drunk on their blood. A great host of warriors stand knee-deep in the carnage, their weapons slick, their faces rapturous. They are not men, not beasts, but something *else*—something made for slaughter. And in their midst, a singular figure stands, wreathed in shadow, its face indistinct… yet somehow *familiar*.

Then, another vision.

His mother, *Mara*, kneeling before an altar, her shoulders hunched, whispering a prayer through trembling lips. Blood stains her hands. A child wails in the background.

*"No… please… I have done as you asked… he is safe… I swear it."*

The voice sneers, thick with cruel amusement.

**"Safe? Look at him. Look at what he has become."**

Drenval’s vision shifts again.

He sees himself—clad in his Hellrider armor, bathed in the golden light of Helm. A paragon of duty. A champion of order.

Then, that light *flickers*.

His form begins to *crack*. His armor darkens, his eyes burn red, and his lips curl into a snarl of utter *rage*. The golden symbol of Helm on his chest corrodes, the metal twisting and reshaping into the emblem of a bloody skull.

**"You wear their chains. You whisper their prayers. But I see your soul, Drenval. I see what you *could* be."**

A presence looms behind him. Cold. Overwhelming. A god made of shadow and steel.

**"You are not weak. You are not afraid. You are *mine*."**

The altar beneath him quivers as his body convulses, sweat rolling down his skin. The *Crimson Rose* is a beacon now, so bright it is unbearable, filling the chamber with its scarlet glow.

His breath is ragged. His pulse, a war drum in his ears.

*"I have watched you. I have waited."*

Each word is a blade carving into his resolve.

*"Show me, Drenval. Show me you are worthy."*

The *Crimson Rose* flares. The hunger inside him grows, *sharpens*. His heart pounds as the temptation takes root.

*"Let your blade carve truth into the world."*

*"Let your hands shape destiny."*

*"Do this… and you shall know what they have hidden from you."*

His father. A face he has never truly known. A voice, muffled, speaking words that send shivers down his spine. *"The blood calls. It always calls."*

The voice grows softer, curling around him like a snake:

*"Do not disappoint me."*

Darkness.

Silence.

And then—air.

Drenval gasps, his lungs burning as if he had been drowning. His knees slam against the cold stone of the dungeon floor.

The *Crimson Rose* is still in his grip, but its glow is dimming, the petals slick with something warm. *Blood.* His blood.

His hands shake. His chest heaves.

Something *is wrong*.

The hunger still lingers, gnawing at the edges of his mind, whispering, whispering, *whispering*.

And then, the scent of blood—*fresh* blood.

# Xebeche (Mikas)

Originally the Tief was a child of a Tiefling father and a Cambion mother in Avernus, of which a demonic and evil game was to experiment with this useless child on how it would fare in Faerun when placed into a tribe of native people. And so the devils orphaned this child to the barbarians of Uthgardt that settled in the Fields of the Dead. Due to his strange appearance, he was seen as a mix of an Uthgardt and a Reghed which was not respected culturally.   
He was often left alone as a child, to which he decided to stalk elk in hopes to prove that he would become a good hunter. Finally one day, the elk spirits laid pity on him, and a young elk gave its life to him. While preparing to cut its meat, city people came upon him, and as he attacked he was knocked out and kidnapped. Later the child awoke going west inside of a cage, sold to a circus. He was an exhibit, but as he mimicked the people around him integrating, the people would lose interest, while the Hellriders saw potential based on the stories of a survivalist child, so they purchased him and trained the child to become a follower of Helm, and a protector in the ranks.   
During his early adult life, the nameless recruit named “Nobody” (as he explained he still had no name given) felt the feeling of a true homesickness and nostalgia for his old life \- visited his old tribe in the night while on a watch with the Hellriders. He would try to teach his fellow tribesmen on his newly found beliefs, but he was rejected for it, and they finally gave him the name of Xebeche, which in the native tongue means “He Who Talks Loud, Saying Nothing”. Rejected from his original guardians for his new beliefs, he returned to Elturel to continue his work with the Hellriders, where seeks purpose in his life, being outcast from 1\. his early life, not feeling at place 2\. in his present with the Hellriders, and 3\. unaware of his true demonic origin.  
![][image3]

Fears:

1. Generally fearful of the civilized world's tricks a.k.a. events or actions you cannot explain or understand  
2. The wrath of diety Helm if disobedient to the Hellriders (character not against this idea where appropriate)  
3. Clockwork beings. It just developed as a fear while reading about them

# Tab 7

# **🔥 Xebeche's Revised Infernal Heritage and Expanded Backstory**

## **🌑 Born of Infernal Games**

Xebeche was born in torment and shaped by cruelty. Conceived within the flaming, iron fortresses of **Avernus**, the first layer of the Nine Hells, he was the offspring of two infernal souls—a Tiefling father, enslaved for his failures and weakness, and a Cambion mother, cruel and ambitious, serving the great Archdevil Zariel. The child's birth was a grim amusement to the devils—seen as nothing more than a pointless, twisted experiment. Devils whispered dark wagers about how such a creature, an impurity of demonic and devilish blood, would fare if cast out into the harsh mortal realms of Faerûn.

As a perverse amusement, the fiends branded him with infernal marks to bind and track him, then hurled him through planar gates onto the mortal plane—**orphaned deliberately** among the proud, barbaric tribes of the **Uthgardt**, settled around the haunted **Fields of the Dead**.

---

## **🌲 An Orphan of Two Worlds**

The Uthgardt tribesmen who found the infernal orphan first recoiled in horror. His visage marked him as alien and strange: his horns curled sharply, skin darkened and smoldering eyes that seemed to hold unspoken malice. Neither fully Uthgardt nor Reghed, he was treated as cursed—a shameful blending that violated deeply-held cultural taboos. Labeled as "useless," the child was ignored, isolated, and left to fend for himself at the fringes of tribal life.

Desperate to prove his worth, Xebeche turned to stalking elk—revered beasts seen as symbols of strength and wisdom by the tribes. Night after lonely night, he watched from shadows, learning the ways of the wild, hoping desperately to show the tribe he belonged among them.

---

## **🦌 Gift from the Spirits**

One fateful dusk, the spirit of the Elk, ancient guardian of these lands, watched the forlorn child with pity. Touched by his persistent bravery, a young elk stepped willingly from the herd, offering itself as sacrifice. Deeply moved yet fiercely determined, Xebeche accepted this offering reverently, prepared to return to the tribe a worthy hunter.

Yet before he could claim this victory, fate struck again. A party of city folk, traveling merchants of dubious intentions, stumbled upon the scene. Frightened and startled, young Xebeche lashed out fiercely, but inexperienced and ill-equipped, he was easily overpowered and rendered unconscious.

---

## **🎪 Life as a Circus Exhibit**

He awoke caged, bound westward toward the bustling cities of Sword Coast civilization. Sold as an exotic curiosity, the nameless orphan became a freakish sideshow—advertised crudely as the "Infernal Wildchild," a strange hybrid creature from the barbarian north. In captivity, he watched, learned, and adapted. Mimicking the accents and manners of his gawkers, he gradually became less of an exotic spectacle and more mundane, an uninteresting and forgotten display.

But fate again intervened. Stories of the wild child reached the ears of **Elturel’s Hellriders**, famed for their discipline, valor, and devout loyalty to **Helm**, god of guardians and protectors. Curious and perhaps sensing potential beneath his savage exterior, the Hellriders purchased his freedom, inducting the feral youth into their ranks.

---

## **⚔️ Becoming “Nobody” – The Nameless Hellrider**

Among the Hellriders, he was trained rigorously—his fierce spirit honed, his fiery instincts tempered. But he remained nameless, introducing himself only as "Nobody" whenever questioned. Despite years of camaraderie and training, he felt perpetually alien, his inner self restless and incomplete. He served loyally, yet a deep yearning pulled at him—a powerful longing, though nameless, for something distant and lost.

During lonely watches, under starlit skies, vague and unsettling dreams haunted him—visions of fire and twisted iron, fleeting glimpses of roaring fiends, and strange whispered voices that echoed unsettlingly familiar.

---

## **🌙 Return to Origins & Rejection**

As he grew older, these pangs of homesickness became unbearable. Finally, under cover of night and without leave, he journeyed back north to the Fields of the Dead, seeking reconnection with the tribe who had first sheltered and then rejected him. He sought to impart the teachings of Helm and share his newfound beliefs.

But the Uthgardt, staunch traditionalists, viewed his alien faith as betrayal and weakness. They scornfully named him **"Xebeche,"** a sharp insult in their native tongue meaning: **“He Who Talks Loud, Saying Nothing.”** Humiliated and estranged again, rejected by the very people he had once fought so hard to impress, he returned bitterly to Elturel, feeling more adrift than ever.

---

## **🔥 Haunted by Hidden Memories**

Though he returned to the Hellriders’ disciplined ranks, Xebeche carried the heavy weight of triple rejection:

* **From his childhood tribe:** mocked and outcast for his mixed heritage.

* **From his new home among the Hellriders:** respected but never truly accepted, always the outsider.

* **From his unknown origin:** haunted by dreams, unaware of his infernal birthright, yet inexplicably attuned to devils and infernal magics.

Each encounter with fiends or infernal sorcery ignited flashes of memory—visions of his dark mother, the cruel Cambion, laughing as she marked him; his father, the broken Tiefling, begging helplessly for mercy; and infernal legions marching beneath burning skies of Avernus. Yet these insights remained confusing, fragmented—tantalizing glimpses without clear meaning.

---

## **🗝️ Questing & Rediscovering Himself**

Each tribal quest that impacts his infernal mark gradually unlocks clearer memories and darker knowledge, piecing together the reality of his origin. Slowly, Xebeche realizes that he's more than just a lost orphan—he is a survivor of cruelty, a child of infernal bloodlines, and potentially, a bridge between mortal honor and infernal power.

The Elk spirit that once pitied him, the very symbol of strength and wisdom he aspired to embody, now represents his own inner struggle. Just as he wrestles between mortal honor and infernal legacy, so too must he choose between succumbing to the burning rage of his origins, or rising above them to forge his own destiny.

---

## **🌅 His Ultimate Journey**

Xebeche's story, at its heart, is one of profound identity and redemption. It’s a saga about discovering and reclaiming who he truly is—no longer an outcast, circus sideshow, nameless soldier, or branded experiment, but someone whose strength lies precisely in the complexity of his past and the purity of his choices.

This realization guides his every action and choice moving forward. Can Xebeche embrace his infernal legacy without losing his mortal heart? Can he find acceptance and purpose in a world that’s repeatedly rejected him?

These questions form the essence of his personal saga.

---

## **📷 Midjourney Image Prompts for Xebeche's Story**

* `Infernal child abandoned by devils among a barbarian tribe in bleak, stormy Fields of the Dead—moody, fantasy realism.`

* `Young Tiefling stalking majestic elk at twilight, hopeful yet forlorn—fantasy art, emotional depth, dynamic lighting.`

* `Circus cage holding infernal child, surrounded by curious gawkers—emotional, gritty realism.`

* `Hellrider training under Elturel's sunlit battlements, disciplined yet lonely Tiefling recruit stands apart—dramatic, immersive scene.`

* `Confrontation with tribal elders under moonlit skies, marked with rejection—intense, fantasy drama.`

---

This rich backstory faithfully integrates your original elements with additional emotional depth and lore-appropriate detail, providing countless narrative hooks and dramatic potential for role-playing and storytelling.

# Aurora Windsnap (Joris)

Background: Aurora "Rory" Windsnap  
Race: Astral Elf  
Class: Wizard (Abjuration)  
Alignment: Chaotic Good

### Early Life:

Aurora "Rory" Windsnap grew up with her head in the clouds—sometimes literally. As a child, she spent countless hours staring into the starry skies, tracing constellations and murmuring to the celestial whispers only she could hear. Her parents, a bard and a tinkerer, fostered her peculiarities, even when they resulted in strange outcomes—like the time she accidentally warded their home so thoroughly that it took two days to open the front door.

Recognizing her talent, Rory’s family encouraged her to study magic formally. She eagerly enrolled at the magical academies of Baldur’s Gate, ready to refine her burgeoning skills.

The Calamity at the Academy:  
Rory’s years at the academy were as eventful as they were brief. While undeniably talented, her tendency to experiment wildly with abjuration magic earned her both admiration and frustration. She often referred to her protective wards as "dear friends" and claimed to see sprites from the Feywild flitting about during lessons.

Her expulsion was the result of one such experiment. During an advanced abjuration class, Rory attempted to enhance a banishment spell, inadvertently sending her professor to the Feywild for three weeks. Though the professor eventually returned—slightly dazed and oddly fond of flowers—the academy decided that Rory’s brilliance was better explored... elsewhere.

Ever the optimist, Rory viewed her expulsion as an opportunity to pursue her studies without the constraints of rigid academia.

Wardrick and the Feywild Cat:  
Rory’s beloved spellbook, Wardrick, was more than a tome of spells—it was her confidant, her diary, and, as Rory insists, perhaps even alive. They shared a bond stronger than words, and she confided in Wardrick about everything—well, almost everything. That’s because Rory’s second-best friend is her familiar: a Feywild cat named Whiskerbright.

Whiskerbright is a sleek, shimmering feline with fur that seems to shift colors like an opal under moonlight. The cat embodies the playful mischief of the Feywild, occasionally disappearing for days on end only to return with strange trinkets or riddles. Rory adores Whiskerbright, often chatting with the familiar as if it were a fellow scholar. She frequently resummons Whiskerbright if it vanishes, though she always makes sure to keep this secret from Wardrick—“I wouldn’t want them to feel jealous,” she says.

The Theft of Wardrick:  
Rory’s tranquil life of magical study took a chaotic turn when she encountered Sees-All-Colors, a Tabaxi claiming to have traveled from the past. He spun wild tales of an apocalypse in Icewind Dale, a frozen explosion that shattered time itself, and his mission to repair the future by saving the past. Rory found his riddles fascinating—until he stole Wardrick and vanished.

Now, Rory is determined to track down the enigmatic Tabaxi, reclaim Wardrick, and maybe figure out what all his talk of “the threads of time” actually means.

Personality:  
Rory exudes a whimsical charm, her unshakable optimism and curiosity making her both endearing and exasperating. She treats magic like an art form, weaving it with intuition and a touch of mischief. Though her Feywild connection makes her seem ungrounded, Rory is fiercely loyal to her friends, whether they’re people, books, or magical cats.

Quirks:  
She often pauses mid-conversation to stare at seemingly empty spaces, claiming she’s watching sprites from the Feywild.  
Rory has been known to hold long debates with Whiskerbright about the ethics of magical wards.  
She hums lullabies to her spells, convinced it helps them “feel more cooperative.”  
Collects mundane objects like buttons and feathers, saying they’re “important fragments of forgotten tales.”  
Refers to Wardrick as her “first best friend,” though she insists Whiskerbright doesn’t mind being second.  
Goals:  
Rory is focused on recovering Wardrick and unraveling the mysteries surrounding Sees-All-Colors’ apocalyptic warnings. She’s equally driven to master abjuration magic, aiming to create wards so powerful they could shield the entire world if needed. Beyond her lofty ambitions, Rory remains deeply curious about the Feywild and its connection to her magic—and perhaps even to her destiny.

Character Hooks:  
Whiskerbright’s mischievous nature often gets Rory into trouble, leaving clues or leading her to unexpected (and sometimes dangerous) places.  
The Feywild professor banishment mishap has left Rory with occasional, unintentional connections to the plane, sometimes manifesting as dreams or brief planar overlaps.  
Rory is drawn to ancient places and forgotten magics, believing they might hold the key to unlocking her full potential—and finding Wardrick.

* nu tai pagal backstory mazdaug

* ### **13:51**

* ### **\]**

* **new**

* ### **\[**

* ### **13:52**

* ### **\]**

* ### **aiceo**

* ### **:**

*  for the same reason

### 

### **Aurora's Family and Academy in Baldur’s Gate**

#### **Magical Academy: Verdashir Academy**

Aurora attended **Verdashir Academy (also known as the Dervish Academy)**, located in **Little Calimshan** in Baldur’s Gate​Baldur's Gate \- Descent…. This school is known for its **advanced spellcasting techniques** and **martial disciplines**, making it an elite institution for arcane studies. The academy primarily trains students in **Calishite magical traditions**, but its library houses **rare tomes on abjuration magic**—a key focus for Aurora.

The academy is **semi-restrictive**, allowing **only the most talented outsiders** to access its archives or join in lessons. Aurora, with her **noble status and powerful abjuration skills**, likely earned a spot but may have been viewed as an **eccentric outlier** due to her fey-touched magic.

* **Exact Location**: Little Calimshan, within the **inner fortified district**.  
* **Notable Features**:  
  * A **hidden courtyard** for advanced training.  
  * A **vaulted archive** with forbidden magical texts.  
  * Secret **planar study rooms**—perhaps where Aurora’s **banishment experiment went wrong**.

---

#### **Noble Residence: Manorborn District**

Aurora’s family resides in the **Manorborn District** of **Upper City**, where the most **influential and wealthy families** live​Baldur's Gate \- Descent…. This district is known for **climbing gardens, fountained courtyards, and private orchards**, showcasing the wealth and refinement of its inhabitants.

##### **Family Reputation:**

* **Arcane Patrons**: Her family has long been involved with **arcane research and magical artifact curation**, making them **valuable patrons of magical development** in Baldur’s Gate.  
* **Ties to the High House of Wonders**: They are frequent donors to **Gond’s High House of Wonders**, where magical inventions and research take place​Baldur's Gate \- Descent….  
* **Trade Connections**: The family has **exclusive trade agreements** for importing **rare magical artifacts** from distant regions, which is how they acquired the **Wand of Celestial Warding** from **The Dale**.  
* **Exact Location**: Upper City, **west side** of Manorborn, near other influential **Parliament of Peers members**.  
* **Notable Features**:  
  * A **sealed vault** rumored to contain **Feywild artifacts**.  
  * A **family crest** resembling an **astral star**, reflecting their celestial ties.  
  * A **hidden passage** connecting to a **noble-only study hall**, where aristocratic mages gather.

## **🔷 Family Members (Noble House Windsnap)**

**House Reputation:** Renowned **magical patrons and researchers**, importing rare **arcane artifacts** and funding magical development. They have strong **political influence** in Baldur’s Gate.

### **1\. Lady Celestia Windsnap (Mother)**

* **Race/Class:** High Elf **(Abjurer Wizard, Noble Scholar)**  
* **Personality:** Stern but devoted; holds **high expectations** for Aurora. She sees **Aurora’s abjuration talents as both a blessing and a curse**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** Celestia was **pregnant with Aurora during the Feywild expedition**. The **Moonkite's celestial energy** may have affected her during that time.  
* **Conflict Hook:** She **opposed Aurora’s expulsion** but later **cut ties** to preserve the family’s **noble reputation**.

### **2\. Lord Dorian Windsnap (Father)**

* **Race/Class:** Human **(Artificer, Noble Inventor)**  
* **Personality:** Warm-hearted but obsessed with **pushing magical boundaries**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** He imported the **Wand of Celestial Warding** from **The Dale**. **He doesn’t know that the wand is linked to the Moonkite**.  
* **Conflict Hook:** If Aurora discovers that her **father’s dealings** led to the Moonkite’s imprisonment, will she **confront him**?

### **3\. Seraphina Windsnap (Younger Sister)**

* **Race/Class:** Half-Elf **(Bard, Court Performer)**  
* **Personality:** Mischievous, dramatic, and **idolizes Aurora** but resents her **for tarnishing the family name**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** She secretly **studies Aurora’s spellbook, Wardrick**, hoping to “**restore**” their family honor.  
* **Conflict Hook:** Seraphina is **friends with House Valtheras**, a **rival noble house** that may **oppose Aurora**.

---

## **🔷 Verdashir Academy (Little Calimshan)**

Aurora studied here before her **banishment mishap**. The academy blends **Calishite magical traditions with advanced arcane research**.

### **4\. Headmistress Ishmira Vedaan (Academy Head)**

* **Race/Class:** Half-Elf **(Diviner Wizard, Headmistress)**  
* **Personality:** **Composed and pragmatic**, but **deeply disappointed in Aurora’s recklessness**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** Ishmira was the **one who had to sign off on her expulsion**.  
* **Conflict Hook:** If Aurora returns to **Baldur’s Gate**, Ishmira might **test her resolve** before allowing her **access to Verdashir’s archives**.

### **5\. Professor Eldrin Thornveil (Former Mentor)**

* **Race/Class:** Tiefling **(Abjurer Wizard, Exiled Scholar)**  
* **Personality:** **Eccentric**, with a deep fascination for **Feywild magic and planar instability**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** He was **banished to the Feywild because of Aurora’s experiment** but returned **years later**.  
* **Conflict Hook:** He **gifted** the **Wand of Celestial Warding** to Aurora, believing it to be **her destiny**.

---

## **🔷 House Valtheras (Rival Nobles)**

House Valtheras opposes Aurora’s family due to their **political influence and arcane research dominance**.

### **6\. Lord Cassiel Valtheras (Rival Patriarch)**

* **Race/Class:** Human **(Warlock, Infernal Pact)**  
* **Personality:** **Cold, calculating, and politically ruthless**.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** Cassiel **opposed House Windsnap’s dealings with celestial magic**, instead favoring **infernal trade**.  
* **Conflict Hook:** He **knows about the Moonkite’s imprisonment** and sees **Aurora’s actions as a threat**.

### **7\. Lady Lysara Valtheras (Aurora’s Noble Rival)**

* **Race/Class:** Half-Elf **(Sorcerer, Noble Enchantress)**  
* **Personality:** **Cunning, graceful, and secretly jealous** of Aurora’s raw power.  
* **Notable Tie to Aurora:** She was Aurora’s **peer and academic rival**, excelling in **controlled magic** while **Aurora’s was wild**.  
* **Conflict Hook:** She might **sabotage** Aurora’s return to Baldur’s Gate.

---

## **🔷 Key Hooks and Relationships**

1. **Family Conflict** → Can Aurora **regain her family’s trust**, or will she **break away** to forge her **own destiny**?  
2. **Academy Ties** → Will Aurora return to **redeem herself**, or is Verdashir **lost to her forever**?  
3. **Rivalry with House Valtheras** → If they **know about the Moonkite**, **Aurora’s actions** could have **political consequences**.  
4. **Father’s Secret** → Did **Lord Dorian Windsnap’s dealings** directly lead to the Moonkite’s suffering?

### **🔹 Sylwenna, the Gloaming Veil**

* **Race/Class:** Eladrin (Autumn) **(Archfey, Abjuration & Dream Magic)**  
* **Personality:** **Calm, enigmatic, and poetic. She speaks in riddles and half-truths.**  
* **Appearance:** Her golden-red hair **shifts with the season**, her cloak billows like a curtain of **twilight mist**, and her eyes shimmer like **dying embers**.  
* **Realm:** She is a **lesser Archfey** of the **Gloaming Veil**, a place between the **Feywild and Astral Plane**, where **celestial-fey energies coalesce**.  
* **Power:** Sylwenna is a **keeper of lost things**—including **Aurora’s celestial fate**.

**🔹 Notable Ties to Aurora:**

* She was **present when the Moonkite was wounded** and **torn into Avernus**.  
* She has been **visiting Aurora in dreams**, whispering **warnings veiled as riddles**.  
* She knows **Aurora is the only one who can free the Moonkite**, but will not say *why*.  
* She has **visited Lady Celestia Windsnap (Aurora’s mother) in the past**.

**🔹 Conflict Hooks:**

* Sylwenna does **not act without purpose**—is she **helping Aurora** out of kindness, or does she **need the Moonkite freed for her own Feywild purposes**?  
* If **Aurora ignores the pull to Avernus**, Sylwenna might **send more direct visions**, perhaps **pulling her into the Feywild temporarily** to force her to listen.  
* She has **agents** in Baldur’s Gate, including **nobles unknowingly bound to her fey bargains**.

---

### **🔹 Lady Celestia Windsnap Knows More**

Aurora’s **mother, Lady Celestia**, has **long known** that something **was different** about Aurora. While she never told her daughter the full truth, she **was not ignorant** of what happened in the Feywild.

**🔹 What She Knows:**

* Celestia **remembers encountering the Moonkite** before Aurora’s birth.  
* She **felt something change within her womb**—a surge of **abjuration magic** unlike anything before.  
* **Sylwenna visited her** shortly after, warning that **Aurora was “woven into the tapestry of the lost”**.  
* **Celestia kept silent**, believing that **hiding the truth was safer**.

**🔹 Conflict Hooks:**

* If Aurora **pressures her**, Celestia may **finally reveal what she knows**.  
* She may **warn Aurora to stay away from Avernus**, believing that **the Moonkite is lost forever**.  
* However, she may also **be hiding more**—was she involved in **getting the Wand of Celestial Warding shipped from The Dale** because she **hoped it would suppress Aurora’s connection**?

---

### **Final Narrative Threads**

* **Sylwenna’s motivations are unclear**—is she an **ally or manipulator**?  
* **Celestia’s silence** may frustrate Aurora, but she did so **to protect her**.  
* **The truth about Aurora’s connection to the Moonkite is still hidden**—Sylwenna and Celestia **know parts of the truth**, but **neither has told her everything**.

# Warlock Aurora

## **🌑 Tier 1 – Faint Whispers (Power Stirring)**

*(Aurora still fragile, only Wizard 2\. The voice is soft, like a lullaby half-heard.)*

* *“Do not falter, little star… even shadows bow to silver light.”*

* *“Raise your hand — the moon guides your aim.”* (Aurora casts Eldritch Blast without realizing it.)

* *“Armor is not iron, but resolve. Stand tall, and you are shielded.”*

* *“Run, if you must — the herd runs with you, swift and unseen.”*

---

## **🌘 Tier 2 – Urgent Guidance (Fey Step & Spells Emerging)**

*(Her whispers grow clearer, as if right behind Aurora’s ear. She gives specific aid when Aurora protects others.)*

* *“Not for yourself alone — leap, and I will carry you.”* (triggers Misty Step)

* *“Still the heart of the foe, as you would soothe a frightened foal.”* (Hold Person / Calm Emotions)

* *“Silver fire burns not to kill, but to guide — let them see the truth.”* (Faerie Fire manifests)

* *“Your mind is a lantern, and no storm can snuff it out.”* (Eldritch Mind protecting concentration)

---

## **🌕 Tier 3 – Claim of the Patron (Full Power Unlock)**

*(In a dire moment — near death or striking the corrupted avatar — her voice becomes clear and resonant, as if spoken by a being standing right behind Aurora.)*

* *“Enough hiding. You are mine, and I am yours — champion and companion.”*

* *“Take up the horn of silver and the bow of starlight. Let them feel what it is to stand against joy unbroken.”*

* *“No curse shall cling to you, nor to those under your care. Banish the dark, Aurora.”* (Remove Curse awakens)

* *“You are not a pawn of fate… you are the rider. Now gallop.”*

---

## **🎭 Whispering Style**

* They should feel like **half-riddles** — guidance wrapped in metaphor.

* At first, **only Aurora hears them**; later, the whole party might notice faint echoes (like bells, hooves, or laughter in the air) when she casts.

* The corrupted Lurue might hiss over them, snarling: *“SILENCE, pretender\! She belongs to the dark\!”* — which makes the whispers more urgent.

---

Do you want me to script a **short mini-scene** where Aurora first hears the whispers mid-fight (Tier 1), so you can drop it word-for-word at the table to set the tone?

You wake. The world is soft and wrong, as if the edges of things have been rubbed away. There is light—silver, flickering, the kind that feels like a memory more than a lantern. You remember pain. You remember falling. And then—silence. Now breath returns to you, shallow and uncertain. Your hands reach for familiar shapes of power, and nothing answers. The patterns scatter. The shield that once wrapped you is gone. There is a hollow in your chest where certainty used to be, and in that hollow something restless stirs. When you close your eyes you hear distant hooves, muffled as if through water. Sometimes a scent of wildflowers or honey drifts past, though no one else seems to notice. Thoughts slip sideways, catching on whispers that fade when you try to hold them. A phrase lingers—*do not falter*—but it feels borrowed, not your own. The more you reach, the more you understand: your knowledge has been pared away, cut down to a fragile core. You are not what you were. Yet in the emptiness, something waits. Not dark, not cruel, but strange and patient, like a hand outstretched through fog. It does not demand, only lingers, as if it already knows your answer. The question is not what you have lost. The question is when you will listen.

# Tier1

# **🌑 Tier 1 – The First Scent**

**Trigger:** The cultists notice Aurora in the melee and immediately focus her with snarling joy, calling her “moon-tainted.”  
 **Read-Aloud:**  
 As their eyes fall on you, something shifts — their lips curl, their nostrils flare, as if they’ve caught a scent on the wind. One breaks from the pack, snarling, *“The prey of the Moon-Foe\! The scent of unicorn\!”* Their blades are all suddenly for you. You stumble, breath failing, and then you hear it — hooves, faint, far away. A whisper curls at the edge of your mind:  
 *“Do not falter, little star…”*  
 Your hand flares with silver light, lashing out before you even think.

**Unlocks:** Eldritch Blast \+ Pact of the Blade (a spectral bow or staff manifests once).

---

# Tier2

# **🌘 Tier 2 – The Herd Gathers**

**Trigger:** An ally falls to the cultists, or Aurora herself is bloodied (below half HP). The cult redoubles their hunting cries: *“Bring her down for the Hunter\!”*  
 **Read-Aloud:**  
 The world tilts — the battle blurs into streaks of color, as if the air itself can’t hold its shape. Silver petals spiral around you, vanishing into motes. The hooves return, louder, closer, beating in rhythm with your heart. A voice breaks through, not faint but fierce:  
 *“Not for yourself alone — leap, and I will carry you.”*  
 Your vision twists — suddenly you are somewhere else, untouched, the reek of blood left behind. The hunters recoil, their eyes narrowing as though they see a shadow running with you — not alone, but a herd.

**Unlocks:** Misty Step (Fey Step uses), Charm Person, Hold Person, Eldritch Mind invocation.

# Tier3

# **🌕 Tier 3 – The Patron’s Claim**

**Trigger:** The cultists corner Aurora, or she’s about to fall unconscious. Their leader raises a blade, snarling, *“The Hunter takes you\!”*  
 **Read-Aloud:**  
 The strike falls — but the world shatters like glass. The battlefield drowns in the thunder of hooves. Antlers of silver fire blaze above you, overlaying your form. Every cultist reels back in instinctive terror, crying out, *“Lurue\! The Moon-Bane herself\!”*  
 The voice is no longer a whisper but a song that shakes your bones:  
 *“They smell me on you. Let them choke on it. Rise, little star — my champion, my herd. They will never take you.”*  
 Light floods your veins, radiant and wild. You *know* now: the power is yours, and it is not borrowed — it is given.

**Unlocks:** Full Fey Warlock Pact (Thirsting Blade, Eldritch Smite, Remove Curse, invisibility, etc.).

# Franceska Riverun

# **📜 Expert Sidekick (Level 4\)**

*Medium Humanoid (Any Race), Typically Neutral*

**Armor Class:** 15 (Leather Armor, 18 DEX)  
 **Hit Points:** 27 (4d8 \+ 8\)  
 **Speed:** 30 ft.

| STR | DEX | CON | INT | WIS | CHA |
| ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- | ----- |
| 10 (+0) | **18 (+4)** | 14 (+2) | 12 (+1) | 14 (+2) | 13 (+1) |

**Saving Throws:** Dex \+6, Wis \+4  
 **Skills:** Stealth \+6, Investigation \+3, Acrobatics \+6, Perception \+4, Persuasion \+3, Sleight of Hand \+6  
 **Senses:** Passive Perception 14  
 **Languages:** Any two languages of choice  
 **Proficiency Bonus:** \+2

---

## **🛠️ Sidekick Features (Level 4\)**

* **Help Action (Bonus Action).** The Expert can use the **Help** action as a **bonus action**, assisting an ally in attacking a creature within 30 feet or aiding them in an ability check.  
* **Cunning Action.** The Expert can **Dash, Disengage, or Hide** as a **bonus action** on each of its turns.  
* **Expertise.** The Expert chooses two skill proficiencies to **double** their proficiency bonus (**Stealth** and **Investigation** in this example).  
* **Reliable Talent (Level 4 Feature).** When the Expert makes an ability check with a skill it is proficient in, a roll of **9 or lower is treated as a 10** before adding bonuses.

---

## **🗡️ Actions**

* **Dagger.** *Melee or Ranged Weapon Attack:* **\+6 to hit**, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target.

  * *Hit:* **7 (1d4+4) piercing damage.**  
* **Shortbow.** *Ranged Weapon Attack:* **\+6 to hit**, range 80/320 ft., one target.

  * *Hit:* **8 (1d6+4) piercing damage.**  
* **Distracting Maneuver (Recharge 5-6).** The Expert feints or misdirects, forcing an enemy within 30 feet to make a **DC 12 Wisdom saving throw**. On failure, the enemy has **disadvantage** on its next attack roll.

---

## **🎒 Equipment**

* Leather Armor  
* Dagger  
* Shortbow (20 Arrows)  
* Thieves’ Tools  
* Disguise Kit  
* A small pouch with **15 gp**

---

## **📌 Notes on Improvements**

✔ **Higher AC (Now 15 instead of 14\)** – Harder to hit\!  
 ✔ **Better Attack Rolls (+6 instead of \+5)** – More accuracy\!  
 ✔ **Increased Damage (+4 DEX bonus to attacks)** – Hits harder\!  
 ✔ **Better Stealth, Acrobatics, and Sleight of Hand (+6 instead of \+5)** – More skilled in sneaky and agile maneuvers\!

---

💡 **Next Steps?** Do you want to add:  
 ✅ A **name**?  
 ✅ A **personality** or **quirks**?  
 ✅ A **backstory** to fit your campaign?

Let me know, and I can help flesh her out\! 😊

# Nico Reid

Name: Nico Reid  
Backstory:  
Summary:  
Lived as a traveling musician. Made a deal with a devil. It all went horribly, I felt very guilty. Joined the Flaming Fist, trusted their judgement and laws. Attended bard college and started looking for a way to atone. Heard about a group that will descend into hells.  
Longer backstory:  
At 14 I was said to be a rising star. That’s what my aunt said, anyway. She wasn’t entirely wrong, but she did believe I had no competition. She was wrong, my competition was something to be reckoned with. (In every festival I performed there was Jack with his awful smile and too low a bow. He always won first place if there was something to win. There was Jasmine too, she beat me on signing onto festivals, even when I hit the road first. Always she performed before me, making everyone think I was simply an interlude before the next great thing.)  
As every foolish young boy I prayed at night to my pillow, or anyone that would listen.  
I want to be the best, I would say.  
Better than them at least, I would whisper.  
I hope they die, I would mutter after their performances.  
I wonder sometimes if he heard me, or if it all was a coincidence. One time a music teacher came to watch our show, Tempo his name was. He praised me, said I could be the next best thing. He then took out his own instrument and what he played was the most horrifyingly beautiful sound I’ve ever heard. He said he would teach me, (give me his instrument?). In return we would battle, our fates resting on the audience. He brought out a contract then, and I knew he was a devil.  
What harm was there to hear him out? I could always refuse, I wouldn’t be foolish. I was 16 then, all my life I have been traveling with musicians and playing on stage. I wouldn’t fall for a devil’s trick. For that is what they did \- trick, fool, find loopholes. That’s what the stories and fables said. That’s what I did too.  
On the paper we wrote down the date and stage of the performance to be held. It was to be performed in a festival in one week. On the festival day I could taste my victory. I could grasp it between my fingertips. I saw my glory, my fame. I had prepared, not only had I practiced for every breathing minute I had had, but I also prepared a trap.  
Changing dates on flyers for the festival was a grueling work with a pencil, but it worked. I placed only my aunt and her dear friends in the audience, I thought myself so clever. My aunt would vote for me of course, she always did. She would coax her friends to do the same. Smugly I winked at the devil as I walked on stage.  
I played my heart out to the small audience of five people. It would be the last song I played before I was famous, I thought. I should have known I had fucked up when the devil smiled stepping onto the stage, instead of fuming with anger.  
Dabar tu pasakyk kas nutiko, nes veikia mano nuomone du budai: 1\. Jis pa-charmino aunt ir jos drauges, ir jos prabalsavo uz ji ir jis pasieme ju sielas \+ jas nuzude.  
2\. As laimejau ir for my glory jis nuzude mano competitors ir pasieme ju sielas.  
Either way, jei 1, tai negalejau gyventi su savo tokia klaida obviously. 2, po menesiu ar keliu the fame meant nothing, it was empty. Every night I saw their faces, I yearned for their challenge, while they screamed burning in hell.  
Toliau:  
Option 1\. Pritaikyciau ir Option 2 lengvai. Galima padaryt my aunt and my brother.  
For hours I stood frozen, my eyes stuck on my aunt’s dismembered body. Legs two chairs to the right, arms one seat to the left and her head in her lap. Then I cried and begged to all gods I knew to bring back time, remake it all, until the actual festival must have began.  
Someone must have called the city watch, the Flaming Fist. It was all a blur of what happened next, but I was too old for an orphanage. I could return to the traveling circus/ music band or I could stay with them and earn my place.  
I locked my violin in my case and left it under my bed for years to come. It was better to trust their laws, their judgement than my own. They were safe, true and just.  
Only I was never really good with a sword, and lousy with everything else. It was Captain XXX (Zodge?) who told me I was useless like this. He told me to attend a bard college, finish what I had planned for my education. There was no life in moping around, but there could be life in revenge, in atoning, and perhaps saving still. So, I did, I dared not sing, but I could dance.  
Somewhere in those days I had life in me again. A library is no place for a bard, but I spent my nights there. Learning, studying. I learned that I could still free their souls (Galiu ane?). I could unmake my mistake.  
After college I returned to the Flaming Fist to serve now as a bard, to pay my student loans too. In the meantime looking for a way into the hells.  
No one should be anything but horrified for Elturel falling into hell. I swear I was not excited. Rather, I was more…. suspicious of what was to come. And when I heard of a party of two hellriders and more in Baldur's Gate on a mission to descend into hells, I practically begged my captain to allow me to tag along.  
Ages:  
Lets say \~16 deal with the devil. Tada two years in flaming fist. 3-4 years college. Now \~24. Galiu sendinti, galiu deal with the devil 18 padaryt.  
Goals:  
Redemption, atonement.  
1\. Find the devil, challenge him to another battle and win. Save my aunt’s (or my competitors’) souls.  
2\. Kadangi “redemption” tai ir do good or anything to achieve it. Like saving Elturel.  
Alignment: kai dariau deal with devil manau buvau chaotic neutral (serving myself). Dabar Lawful good (not trusting my own judgement, tai vadovaujuosi Flaming Fist laws).  
Feats:  
Tavern brawler in tough, because of training and drinking with Flaming Fist recruits. I have learned. xD plius I make unarmed strikes, tai kaip ir fits?  
Personality:  
all smiles and self-mocking charm (kad niekas ne-question-intu ka prisidirbau).  
guarded (when it comes to personal stuff).  
fidgety (high dex).  
Does not trust his own judgement.  
Questions for DM: Ar Flaming Fist priima bardus? Am I a special case?  
Ka zinau apie ta devil, ka buciau galejes issiaiskint?  
Does the devil have my soul too?  
Can I save souls or simply free them from hell?
